jokes.text: I had a smoke detector installed in my ass, and I don't hear any beeping
jokes.setup: Someone says they're not blowing smoke up Larry's ass
jokes.punchline: Larry literally interprets it with an absurd smoke detector installation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on a common idiom, perfectly delivered with technical specificity that makes it funnier. Classic Larry Sanders neurotic literalism.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I had a smoke detector installed in my ass, and I don't hear any beeping

jokes.text: My ass takes 'd' cells. I'll check it later.
jokes.setup: The smoke detector in ass premise established
jokes.punchline: Escalates with specific battery type and casual maintenance plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the smoke detector bit with technical detail and casual delivery that sells the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: My ass takes 'd' cells. I'll check it later.

jokes.text: I'd do it, Hank, but I made plans to put all my cash in a bag and toss it over a cliff
jokes.setup: Hank pitches horse racing investment
jokes.punchline: Artie sarcastically describes equally bad financial decision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie deadpan sarcasm, conveying his opinion of Hank's scheme through exaggerated comparison.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'd do it, Hank, but I made plans to put all my cash in a bag and toss it over a cliff

jokes.text: I can't even afford a bag
jokes.setup: Artie's cliff-tossing bag joke
jokes.punchline: Larry one-ups the poverty with even more specific destitution
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and perfect character moment - Larry's insecurity about money in four words.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I can't even afford a bag

jokes.text: We should just hire a fucking woodpecker for a sidekick
jokes.setup: Frustration with Hank's horse obsession
jokes.punchline: Suggests replacing Hank with equally single-minded animal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Artie insult that perfectly captures his view of Hank as repetitive and one-note.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: We should just hire a fucking woodpecker for a sidekick

jokes.text: Don't think of her as a person. Just think of her as an employee.
jokes.setup: Larry is scared to meet new people
jokes.punchline: Beverly suggests dehumanizing solution matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Classic Beverly dark humor - casually suggesting objectification as practical solution to Larry's neurosis.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Don't think of her as a person. Just think of her as an employee.

jokes.text: Oh! I was just stretching.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if Larry nodded agreement
jokes.punchline: Larry lies about involuntary body language
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's inability to commit, even to a simple nod.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Oh! I was just stretching.

jokes.text: Can't wait to meet your sisters the Pinta and the Santa Maria
jokes.setup: Meeting intern named Nina
jokes.punchline: References Columbus ships for tired historical pun
jokes.explanation: Deliberately hackneyed joke that Larry acknowledges is old - the joke is that it's a bad joke.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Can't wait to meet your sisters the Pinta and the Santa Maria

jokes.text: I've heard that one before. Well, it dates back to 1492. I don't doubt it.
jokes.setup: Larry makes Columbus ships joke
jokes.punchline: Characters acknowledge the joke's staleness
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about the hackneyed nature of the previous joke, showing self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.line: I've heard that one before.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Well, it dates back to 1492.

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.line: I don't doubt it.

jokes.text: Oh! Oh! Why weren't the cameras still rolling?!
jokes.setup: Larry's absurd smoke detector bit just happened
jokes.punchline: Someone lamenting they didn't capture it for TV
jokes.explanation: Meta moment that acknowledges they're on a TV show, commenting on how good Larry's ad-lib was.

dialogue.line: Oh! Oh! Why weren't the cameras still rolling?!

jokes.text: Frasier goes on date. My God in heaven! Don't let me forget to set my VCR!
jokes.setup: Phil explaining TV Guide summaries are ridiculously short
jokes.punchline: Mock excitement over mundane plot description
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about TV Guide brevity, though not particularly sharp or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Frasier goes on date. My God in heaven! Don't let me forget to set my VCR!

jokes.text: Phil, scurry back to your hutch and cogitate a little more, my boy
jokes.setup: Phil presents weak comedy writing
jokes.punchline: Artie dismisses him with elaborate condescension
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie vocabulary flexing - 'scurry,' 'hutch,' and 'cogitate' are perfectly pompous dismissal.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Phil, scurry back to your hutch and cogitate a little more, my boy

jokes.text: If we do this desk piece, the audience will rush the stage and burn down the desk
jokes.setup: Evaluating quality of comedy bit
jokes.punchline: Extreme physical violence as proportional response to bad comedy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of criticism into absurd mob violence imagery, delivered with Artie's trademark seriousness.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: If we do this desk piece, the audience will rush the stage and burn down the desk

jokes.text: Don't tell us! Tell the career suicide hotline! Bye-bye!
jokes.setup: Writer says he's going to SNL
jokes.punchline: Artie suggests specialized suicide hotline for career moves
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept of specialized hotline, perfectly delivered with dismissive 'bye-bye.' Classic Artie cruelty with wit.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Don't tell us! Tell the career suicide hotline! Bye-bye!

jokes.text: Well, that's what separates you from the others. It's a classic. It works, you know?
jokes.setup: Larry repeating the same line about separating from others
jokes.punchline: Larry being proud of reusing the same joke
jokes.explanation: Self-aware callback showing Larry's tendency to recycle material and be proud of it.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, that's what separates you from the others. It's a classic. It works, you know?

jokes.text: What cologne are you wearing? Sand dabs. I don't like to eat them. I just rub them on.
jokes.setup: Hank asks about Larry's cologne
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals he rubs fish on himself instead of eating them
jokes.explanation: Perfectly bizarre Larry Sanders moment - neurotic food/hygiene behavior delivered matter-of-factly. Classic character absurdism.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: What cologne are you wearing?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Sand dabs. I don't like to eat them. I just rub them on.

jokes.text: I am a sick fuck. I'm a sick, sick fuck.
jokes.setup: Nina flirting with Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-aware moral panic
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Larry's internal conflict, though the self-flagellation is fairly predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I am a sick fuck. I'm a sick, sick fuck.

jokes.text: No, you're a great fuck.
jokes.setup: Larry calls himself a sick fuck
jokes.punchline: Nina misinterprets as sexual compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection based on dual meaning of 'fuck,' delivered with innocent enthusiasm.

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.line: No, you're a great fuck.

jokes.text: Because you're just clearly younger than me and yet more together, and I find that depressing.
jokes.setup: Nina asking why Larry wants to know her age
jokes.punchline: Larry's honest assessment of how her maturity threatens his self-image
jokes.explanation: Good Larry self-awareness and insecurity, but somewhat expected sentiment.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Because you're just clearly younger than me and yet more together, and I find that depressing.

jokes.text: Nacho?
jokes.setup: Intimate moment building between Larry and Nina
jokes.punchline: Mundane food offering deflates romantic tension
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation - one word completely undercuts romantic tension with absurd mundanity.

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.line: Nacho?

jokes.text: Promise? Cheese?
jokes.setup: Larry extracting promise of secrecy about their affair
jokes.punchline: Nina casually offers cheese during serious moment
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the nacho deflation, juxtaposing serious conversation with snack offerings.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Promise?

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.line: Cheese?

jokes.text: I don't think so. I didn't adopt her, I just slept with her.
jokes.setup: Comparison to Woody Allen/Soon-Yi situation
jokes.punchline: Larry draws technical distinction about type of inappropriate relationship
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect Larry logic - he finds the technicality that makes his behavior 'better' than Woody Allen's.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I don't think so. I didn't adopt her, I just slept with her.

jokes.text: Wouldn't I like to give her the old pork sausage? Hello, Jimmy Dean. Enh. Enh.
jokes.setup: Hank sees attractive intern
jokes.punchline: Elaborate sexual metaphor with brand name and sound effects
jokes.explanation: Typical Hank crudeness with brand reference, but overly elaborate and not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Wouldn't I like to give her the old pork sausage? Hello, Jimmy Dean. Enh. Enh.

jokes.text: Larry? I wanna thank you for 8 wonderful years. This is my gift to you. And I love you.
jokes.setup: Hank presents horse as gift
jokes.punchline: Overly emotional declaration in inappropriate moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank awkwardness - his desperate need for Larry's approval creates uncomfortable moment.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Larry? I wanna thank you for 8 wonderful years. This is my gift to you. And I love you.

jokes.text: Comes the waterworks. I'm outta here.
jokes.setup: Hank's emotional declaration
jokes.punchline: Artie's immediate exit from uncomfortable situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie reaction - he immediately recognizes and flees emotional moments with perfect timing.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Comes the waterworks. I'm outta here.

jokes.text: If anybody should be fucking her, it should be me. I'm closer to her age than Larry.
jokes.setup: Learning about Larry and Nina's relationship
jokes.punchline: Hank's self-serving mathematical justification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hank's jealousy and twisted logic about appropriateness.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: If anybody should be fucking her, it should be me. I'm closer to her age than Larry.

jokes.text: Between the O.J. verdicts, Showgirls, and Larry dating an 18-year-old, that's it. I'm killing myself.
jokes.setup: Cultural events of 1995
jokes.punchline: Larry's personal life equated with national disasters
jokes.explanation: Excellent cultural reference joke placing Larry's scandal in context of actual 1995 disasters.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Between the O.J. verdicts, Showgirls, and Larry dating an 18-year-old, that's it. I'm killing myself.

jokes.text: And no jury in the world would convict you.
jokes.setup: Beverly threatens suicide over cultural disasters
jokes.punchline: Phil agrees the circumstances justify it
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that extends the O.J. trial reference while supporting Beverly's despair.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: And no jury in the world would convict you.

jokes.text: I can look at anyone, except Victoria Principal, and guess exactly how old they are.
jokes.setup: Phil claims age-guessing talent
jokes.punchline: Random specific exception undermines the claim
jokes.explanation: Decent specificity humor - the random exception makes the talent claim absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: I can look at anyone, except Victoria Principal, and guess exactly how old they are.

jokes.text: I go to see a movie, I guess, probably once every couple weeks. Absolutely. Right. There you go.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about Broadway theater
jokes.punchline: Conversation completely derails into meaningless movie frequency
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Larry's interview skills failing under personal distraction.

dialogue.speaker: John Stamos
dialogue.line: I go to see a movie, I guess, probably once every couple weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Absolutely.

dialogue.speaker: John Stamos
dialogue.line: Right.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: There you go.

jokes.text: You can get alcohol there just in case the show's a little slow.
jokes.setup: Discussing Broadway theater amenities
jokes.punchline: Practical reason for theater bar service
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about theater experience, nothing particularly sharp or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: John Stamos
dialogue.line: You can get alcohol there just in case the show's a little slow.

jokes.text: Are you seeing anyone? What's going on... no. No. No, single. Single. Yes. Dating around? What do you... yeah, well. Yeah. Who you dating? Anyone we know? Anyone... no. No one... no one to speak of.
jokes.setup: John Stamos asking about Larry's dating life on air
jokes.punchline: Larry's stammering, uncomfortable evasion about his secret relationship
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Larry's discomfort with public scrutiny.

dialogue.speaker: John Stamos
dialogue.line: Are you seeing anyone?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What's going on... no. No. No, single. Single. Yes. Dating around? What do you... yeah, well. Yeah. Who you dating? Anyone we know? Anyone... no. No one... no one to speak of.

jokes.text: We've been together 8 years... being sentimental. You know. I just... but in a professional, platonic way.
jokes.setup: Hank touching Artie during emotional song
jokes.punchline: Desperate clarification that physical contact isn't romantic
jokes.explanation: Good Hank awkwardness - his desperate need to clarify platonic intentions makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: We've been together 8 years... being sentimental. You know. I just... but in a professional, platonic way.

jokes.text: You are very close to sexual harassment.
jokes.setup: Hank's inappropriate touching and explanations
jokes.punchline: Artie's matter-of-fact warning about legal consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie deadpan delivery of serious workplace concern in absurd context.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You are very close to sexual harassment.

jokes.text: What the fuck do you think you're doing? It's a beautiful song, and I have feelings, and thank you for ruining it.
jokes.setup: Hank interrupting musical performance with horse talk
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive emotional outburst about his sensitivity
jokes.explanation: Good Larry moment - his artistic pretensions and emotional volatility on full display.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: What the fuck do you think you're doing? It's a beautiful song, and I have feelings, and thank you for ruining it.

jokes.text: I don't give a fuck about a horse. I have a car.
jokes.setup: Argument about horse racing interrupting music
jokes.punchline: Larry's transportation-based dismissal of horses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant non sequitur logic - Larry dismisses gambling because he has alternate transportation. Perfect absurdist reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I don't give a fuck about a horse. I have a car.

jokes.text: Or should I say, glad you could Sta-mos?
jokes.setup: Thanking John Stamos for staying
jokes.punchline: Forced pun on guest's last name
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad host pun showing Larry's desperation to be funny when distracted by personal drama.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Or should I say, glad you could Sta-mos?

jokes.text: This appears to be the Saved by the Bell reunion.
jokes.setup: Larry walks in on young people partying
jokes.punchline: References teen TV show to mock their youth
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference showing Larry's age insecurity, though fairly predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: This appears to be the Saved by the Bell reunion.

jokes.text: Hey, they let O.J. go, didn't they? Come on. Isn't that enough?
jokes.setup: Larry making mess at party
jokes.punchline: Compares minor misbehavior to major criminal case
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier O.J. reference, using cultural moment to excuse personal behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Hey, they let O.J. go, didn't they? Come on. Isn't that enough?

jokes.text: That's good, well, maybe they'll pop for the bill. One can only hope.
jokes.setup: Learning Larry is meeting Nina's parents
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about who pays for dinner
jokes.explanation: Good cynical humor from guest character, deflating romantic situation with financial practicality.

dialogue.speaker: Shawn Colvin
dialogue.line: That's good, well, maybe they'll pop for the bill. One can only hope.

jokes.text: We just lie in bed together and laugh at you.
jokes.setup: Parents explaining they watch Larry's show
jokes.punchline: Innocent statement with unintentionally inappropriate imagery
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable humor - parents inadvertently create awkward mental image while being complimentary.

dialogue.line: We just lie in bed together and laugh at you.

jokes.text: I often read the menu right before I actually order. Otherwise, I find them boring.
jokes.setup: Asked what he's reading
jokes.punchline: Overly literal explanation of menu reading habits
jokes.explanation: Good Larry Sanders logic - finding the least social interpretation of a normal question.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I often read the menu right before I actually order. Otherwise, I find them boring.

jokes.text: He jumped the rail, and he dumped the jockey, and he ran across the infield... he got into the parking lot and out onto Colorado Boulevard.
jokes.setup: Horse was winning the race
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd escape from racetrack to public street
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating physical comedy - horse's escape becomes increasingly elaborate and absurd. Great visual imagery.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: He jumped the rail, and he dumped the jockey, and he ran across the infield... he got into the parking lot and out onto Colorado Boulevard.

jokes.text: I don't know how, but he got into the parking lot and out onto colorado Boulevard.
jokes.setup: Horse escaping the track
jokes.punchline: Horse somehow made it to major street
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalating disaster with geographical specificity that makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I don't know how, but he got into the parking lot and out onto colorado Boulevard.

jokes.text: We should be expecting 2, maybe 3 lawsuits.
jokes.setup: Horse running loose on Colorado Boulevard
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of legal consequences
jokes.explanation: Good final beat of horse story - matter-of-fact delivery of serious consequences.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: We should be expecting 2, maybe 3 lawsuits.

jokes.text: You need to go through a lot of pain to be interesting.
jokes.setup: Beverly explaining why young relationships don't work
jokes.punchline: Pain as prerequisite for personality depth
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark wisdom from Beverly - cynical but arguably true observation about experience and personality.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: You need to go through a lot of pain to be interesting.

jokes.text: I didn't get interesting until after my first marriage, and then I was so fucking interesting, I couldn't get out of bed for a month.
jokes.setup: Beverly's theory about pain making you interesting
jokes.punchline: Larry's personal example where pain made him dysfunctional
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of Beverly's theory - Larry's pain made him so 'interesting' it was debilitating. Perfect dark self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I didn't get interesting until after my first marriage, and then I was so fucking interesting, I couldn't get out of bed for a month.

jokes.text: Fucking John Stamos. I can't believe they're still there.
jokes.setup: Watching Nina laugh with John Stamos
jokes.punchline: Larry's bitter resentment of harmless guest
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's irrational jealousy turning against innocent bystander.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Fucking John Stamos. I can't believe they're still there.

jokes.text: I talked to John Stamos for 11 minutes the other night on the show, and you want... I nearly went into a coma.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Nina is faking enjoyment with Stamos
jokes.punchline: Larry's exaggerated description of boring conversation
jokes.explanation: Good hyperbolic description showing Larry's bias and wounded ego about losing Nina's attention.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I talked to John Stamos for 11 minutes the other night on the show, and you want... I nearly went into a coma.

jokes.text: You're gonna have a great time.
jokes.setup: John Stamos says he's meeting Nina's parents
jokes.punchline: Larry's forced pleasant response to devastating news
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe ending - Larry forced to be gracious while learning he's been completely replaced.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: You're gonna have a great time.
